canonical_question: How do I design a search engine architecture?

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: Elasticsearch 8.0 — security on by default, Lucene 9.x vector search support (2022-02)

constraints: Inverted indexes are write-heavy on ingestion — never colocate indexing and query workloads on the same nodes in production
Shard count is fixed at index creation in Elasticsearch — choose shard count based on expected data volume, not current volume
BM25 relevance scoring assumes term independence — highly correlated terms need phrase queries or shingles
Vector search (kNN) requires dedicated memory budget — each float32 vector of dimension d costs 4*d bytes per document
Segment merges in Lucene are I/O-intensive — schedule force-merges during off-peak hours only
Relevance tuning without A/B testing is guesswork — always instrument click-through rate and nDCG

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Looking for a hosted search-as-a-service solution comparison
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Compare hosted search products — this unit covers build-your-own architecture

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need to debug slow Elasticsearch queries in an existing cluster
Designing a recommendation engine (collaborative filtering, not search)

inputs_needed.question: What is the expected document corpus size and query volume?
What type of search does the system need?
What is the acceptable p99 query latency?
